signals.title: M&A Activity
signals.desc: BMS acquisition speculation drove a notable spike in Jan–Feb conversation volume.

signals.title: Pipeline Momentum
signals.desc: Phase 3 SCOUT-HCM results for Camzyos generated strong positive earned media.

signals.title: AI Partnership
signals.desc: Microsoft AI / lung cancer detection deal elevated innovation narrative.

signals.title: Investor Signal
signals.desc: Scotiabank raised price target to $60 — broad analyst coverage remained favorable.

signals.title: Radio DTC Campaign
signals.desc: 'No Time to Wait' drove 736 SM radio posts but generated 0 digital interactions.

working: LinkedIn engagement is highest of all SM channels (64% of interactions)
Pipeline news generating positive earned media at scale
Institutional analyst coverage broadly favorable across Q1
notWorking: 57% of SM content is neutral — weak brand voice resonance
78% of TM overwhelmingly neutral — minimal earned narrative
Radio DTC (736 posts) generating zero digital interactions

risks.text: 2% negative SM content could amplify if M&A speculation turns hostile
Low engagement on Radio = wasted DTC investment signal

opportunities.text: LinkedIn professional audience underserved in depth content

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: February peaks at 859 posts — a 154% increase over January's 338. This aligns with Q4 earnings release on Jan 28 and subsequent analyst day discussions.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: LinkedIn and Radio constitute 76% of total volume. LinkedIn captures professional/investor discourse; Radio reflects DTC broadcast ("No Time to Wait" campaign).

insights.label: Platform
insights.text: LinkedIn (793), Radio (736), X/Twitter (253), Video (100), Facebook (76) — a diverse channel mix, but engagement concentration is heavily LinkedIn-weighted.

insights.label: Sustainability
insights.text: March sustains at 659 posts (77% of Feb peak), suggesting organic conversation momentum rather than purely event-driven spikes.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: April 2026 (partial data, 133 posts through Apr 6) on track for strong month if ASCO or pipeline announcements land in late April.

kpi-audience.title: SM Total Audience: 291M
kpi-audience.sub: Cumulative reach across all social posts

insights.label: Scale
insights.text: 291 million total audience impressions across 1,989 posts. Radio-tagged posts carry the largest individual audience figures (avg 330K per Radio post).

insights.label: Quality vs Quantity
insights.text: LinkedIn posts have lower audience figures per post but far higher interaction rates. Radio has massive passive reach but zero active engagement.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Radio reach is passive-only — audiences cannot interact, share, or amplify. This inflates the "reach" metric without contributing to brand advocacy or engagement equity.

insights.label: Benchmark
insights.text: For pharma at this scale, 291M impressions across a 90-day window is competitive. Key question is how much converts to message retention vs. passive exposure.

insights.label: Optimization
insights.text: Reallocating 20% of Radio budget to LinkedIn and YouTube could convert passive reach to active engagement, potentially 3–5× improvement in interaction rates.

insights.label: Volume Context
insights.text: 30,000 articles over 3 months averages ~333 articles/day. This reflects the scale of BMS's media footprint — a blue-chip pharma company generating constant financial and clinical news.

insights.label: Structural vs Event
insights.text: Volume is remarkably stable across Jan-Feb-Mar (7.1K / 7.4K / 6.7K), suggesting structural/syndication-driven coverage rather than purely event-triggered spikes.

insights.label: Channel
insights.text: Online (71%) and Web (26%) dominate — traditional print and broadcast represent a very small fraction of total volume but outsized credibility per placement.

insights.label: Reach Implication
insights.text: Most coverage comes from aggregator/syndication sources (PRSync: 522, Market Minute: 120, Local news: 200+). These have high volume but moderate individual reach.

insights.label: Strategic Gap
insights.text: High volume but low editorial depth — most articles are wire-service summaries, not investigative or feature coverage. This limits narrative control.

insights.label: Scale
insights.text: 399 million cumulative reach from TM vastly exceeds SM (291M), confirming traditional media still delivers superior audience scale for pharma.

insights.label: Quality Note
insights.text: Average reach per article (~18,758) is moderate, inflated by high-reach outlets like MSN (71M UMV). Most articles appear in local/aggregator outlets with lower individual reach.

insights.label: Concentration Risk
insights.text: A handful of high-reach outlets (MSN, Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat) generate disproportionate reach. Losing favor with these platforms would meaningfully impact total TM footprint.

insights.label: Earned vs Syndicated
insights.text: Syndicated wire content (PRSync, openpr.com) makes up ~800+ articles and likely drives significant reach volume but limited editorial credibility.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Converting 5-10 major earned editorial placements in Stat News, FiercePharma, or JAMA would add disproportionate credibility vs. volume wins.

insights.label: Rate Context
insights.text: 32.3% positive rate is healthy for a pharma company — industry norms typically run 20-30% positive for large-cap drugmakers. BMS outperforms baseline.

insights.label: Key Drivers
insights.text: Positive posts cluster around: Camzyos clinical success, Microsoft AI partnership, analyst upgrades, oncology conference activity (ASCO preview content).

insights.label: Neutral Concern
insights.text: 57% neutral content reflects information dissemination without emotional resonance. Brand advocacy requires converting neutrals to positives through narrative investment.

insights.label: Negative Monitoring
insights.text: 2% negative (40 posts) is manageable. Key themes: valuation skepticism, generic competition concerns. No single crisis-level narrative detected.

insights.label: Platform Differential
insights.text: LinkedIn positive rate likely higher than Radio; Radio tends toward neutral (product mentions in ad context). Platform-specific sentiment analysis would sharpen strategy.

insights.label: Industry Context
insights.text: 4.5% positive in TM is typical for pharma, where neutral/informational coverage dominates. Financial media in particular skews neutral by editorial standards.

insights.label: Positive Drivers
insights.text: Investment/analyst upgrades (Scotiabank, BMO, Jefferies), clinical trial successes, and M&A opportunity framing drive the positive pool.

insights.label: Gap vs SM
insights.text: SM generates 32% positive vs TM's 4.5% — a 7× gap. Social channels are the primary venue for brand enthusiasm. TM shapes credibility, SM shapes affinity.

insights.label: Negative is Low
insights.text: 1.5% negative (384 articles) indicates no crisis-level events in Q1. Patent concerns and valuation debate generate scattered negatives, not concentrated attacks.

insights.label: Benchmark Target
insights.text: With proactive media engagement targeting financial publications with pipeline wins, TM positive rate target of 6-8% is achievable by Q4 2026.

kpi-interactions.title: SM Interactions: 14,466
kpi-interactions.sub: Likes, shares, comments across all platforms

insights.label: Composition
insights.text: LinkedIn drives 9,354 interactions (64.7%), X/Twitter 1,982 (13.7%), Facebook 1,909 (13.2%), Video 1,192 (8.2%), Instagram minimal.

insights.label: Engagement Rate
insights.text: 14,466 interactions across 291M audience = 0.005% engagement rate. Pharma benchmark is typically 0.01-0.05%. Headroom to improve content quality and calls-to-action.

insights.label: LinkedIn Depth
insights.text: LinkedIn's high interaction count signals engaged professional audience (HCPs, investors, analysts). Long-form content and clinical data receive strongest response.

insights.label: Facebook Surprise
insights.text: Facebook generates 1,909 interactions from only 76 posts (25 interactions/post average) — highest per-post engagement of any channel. Possibly patient/consumer community activity.

insights.label: Amplification Gap
insights.text: Radio (736 posts) contributes 0 interactions. Redirecting 30% of Radio content budget to LinkedIn/Facebook would likely 2-3× total interaction count.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: Q1 2026 saw $47B+ in biopharma M&A activity (Merck $6.7B, Lilly $6.3B, Biogen $5.6B). BMS is repeatedly named alongside Amgen and AbbVie as the next potential dealmaker.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: BMO and Jefferies analyst notes explicitly call out "return of large-scale deal making" and flag BMS as having balance sheet capacity. This amplifies BMS coverage across financial media.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: M&A speculation elevates brand awareness among institutional investors and business media. Use this moment to reinforce BMS strategic vision — position deals as pipeline-driven, not defensive.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: If BMS is seen as reactive or FOMO-driven in M&A, perception could shift negative. Messaging should proactively frame any deal as scientific strategy, not financial engineering.

insights.label: Monitoring Priority
insights.text: Set alerts for: "BMS acquires," "Bristol-Myers deal," analyst notes on BMS capital deployment. Any misinformation needs rapid correction.

insights.label: Clinical Signal
insights.text: Phase 3 SCOUT-HCM positive topline results for Camzyos in adolescent HCM patients generated a wave of clinical media coverage in mid-January 2026.

insights.label: Coverage Quality
insights.text: Coverage spans trade media (Healthcare Research Reports, StreetInsider) and financial media (Yahoo Finance, MSN). Both high-credibility channels for this audience.

insights.label: Brand Lift
insights.text: Clinical success stories convert neutral TM coverage into positive branded narratives. Each Phase 3 win should be exploited across SM within 24-48 hours for maximum amplification.

insights.label: Portfolio Depth
insights.text: BMS pipeline includes 50+ compounds across oncology, hematology, neuroscience, immunology, cardiovascular. This breadth is underutilized in public narrative.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Build a "Pipeline Pipeline" content calendar: map each Phase 2/3 readout in 2026 to a pre-planned social and media outreach moment. Don't let wins pass without brand amplification.

insights.label: Signal
insights.text: January 20 announcement of BMS-Microsoft partnership to enhance early lung cancer detection using AI generated immediate multi-channel coverage.

insights.label: Narrative Value
insights.text: AI partnerships are a high-signal indicator of innovation positioning in 2026. BMS being associated with Microsoft (the AI market leader) elevates brand perception among tech-savvy investors and HCPs.

insights.label: Audience Resonance
insights.text: Oncology professionals and investors both respond strongly to AI-driven diagnostic partnerships. LinkedIn is the ideal channel — professionals trust scientific collaborations here.

insights.label: Competitor Gap
insights.text: Few traditional pharma peers have announced comparable high-profile AI partnerships. BMS has a 6-12 month window to own this narrative before competitors catch up.

insights.label: Content Strategy
insights.text: Develop a "BMS x AI" content series: feature Microsoft collaboration updates, AI-assisted trial design, precision medicine case studies. Target Science, Nature Medicine, STAT News for earned placements.

insights.label: Event
insights.text: January 9 Scotiabank analyst raise to $60 (from $53) was among top 10 most-reached articles in the dataset. Signal cascades: multiple financial outlets syndicate analyst rating changes.

insights.label: Cascade Effect
insights.text: A single positive analyst note generates 50-100+ downstream articles across financial media (American Banking News, Yahoo Finance, FinancialContent, etc.) — this is the TM syndication amplification machine.

insights.label: Sentiment Impact
insights.text: Analyst upgrades are the single most reliable driver of positive TM sentiment for large-cap pharma. Cultivating relationships with bulge-bracket analysts is a high-ROI communications investment.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Analyst sentiment can reverse quickly. If Q2 2026 results disappoint, downgrades would generate equivalent negative cascade. Maintain consistent analyst communications cadence.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: Identify which additional analysts are on the fence (Sector Perform / Hold ratings). Targeted investor day content could move 2-3 analysts to Buy, generating another positive coverage wave.

sig-radio.title: Radio DTC Campaign: No Time to Wait

insights.label: Observation
insights.text: "No Time to Wait" campaign (AFib/stroke awareness, sponsored by BMS & Pfizer) is heavily represented in Radio social media data — 736 posts from FM stations across Florida, Pennsylvania, and other markets.

insights.label: Reach Without Engagement
insights.text: Individual Radio posts show audience figures of 300K-400K each, contributing to total SM reach but generating zero digital interactions. DTC reach is passive.

insights.label: Implication
insights.text: Radio DTC awareness may not be converting to digital search or HCP-directed action. The absence of digital amplification means the campaign lives in broadcast silos.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Budget allocation to Radio without measurable digital ROI is a strategic vulnerability. As audiences continue shifting to digital/streaming, Radio's effectiveness should be questioned.

insights.label: Recommendation
insights.text: Add QR codes, vanity URLs, or social hashtag pushes to radio ads to create digital thread. Retarget radio-exposed audiences through programmatic digital to complete the conversion funnel.

exec-working.title: What's Working: Key Strengths
exec-working.sub: Momentum drivers across SM and TM

insights.label: LinkedIn Dominance
insights.text: LinkedIn generates 64% of all SM interactions from 40% of posts. Professional audience is highly engaged with clinical, scientific, and investment content.

insights.label: Pipeline Resonance
insights.text: Phase 3 clinical data, new compound readouts, and therapy approvals are generating organic earned media that builds genuine brand credibility.

insights.label: Financial Media Coverage
insights.text: Major financial outlets (Yahoo Finance, MSN, StreetInsider) regularly feature BMS news — ensuring institutional investor audiences stay informed and confident.

insights.label: Innovation Narrative
insights.text: The Microsoft AI partnership seeds BMS as a technology-forward pharma. This narrative differentiates BMS from "traditional" drug companies in a competitive landscape.

insights.label: Analyst Consensus
insights.text: Predominantly positive-to-neutral analyst coverage (Scotiabank, BMO, Jefferies all constructive) provides a favorable investment-grade halo that amplifies across media.

insights.label: Neutral Dominance
insights.text: 57% of SM and 78% of TM is neutral — informational without advocacy. Brand coverage is comprehensive but emotionally flat, limiting share-of-mind impact.

insights.label: Radio ROI Gap
insights.text: 736 Radio posts generate 0 digital interactions. A major media investment that creates awareness but no measurable digital engagement, conversions, or brand advocacy.

insights.label: Patient Voice Absent
insights.text: Patient and caregiver perspectives are nearly absent in social media data. For a healthcare company, this is a credibility gap — authentic patient stories drive advocacy and payer support.

insights.label: Low Engagement Rate
insights.text: Overall SM engagement rate of ~0.005% is below pharma benchmarks. Content resonates with a narrow professional audience but fails to achieve broad consumer-grade engagement.

insights.label: Syndication-Heavy TM
insights.text: Most TM coverage is syndicated wire content (PRSync, FinancialContent, etc.) — not original editorial. This means BMS gets volume but limited narrative influence or brand building.

insights.label: M&A Speculation
insights.text: If BMS is involved in leaked or inaccurate acquisition rumors, negative sentiment could spike rapidly. Social media escalation from M&A misinformation can be swift and damaging.

insights.label: LOE/Patent Cliff
insights.text: References to loss of exclusivity and generic competition appear in negative coverage pool. If this narrative gains momentum in H2 2026, financial media could amplify.

insights.label: Analyst Reversal Risk
insights.text: Current positive analyst consensus could reverse on Q2 earnings miss. A cluster of analyst downgrades would trigger a negative TM cascade (same mechanism as the Scotiabank upgrade worked positively).

insights.label: Radio Waste
insights.text: Continued investment in radio DTC without digital conversion tracking risks perpetuating an unproven spend while digital channels demonstrate measurable ROI.

insights.label: Patient Narrative Vacuum
insights.text: Without a proactive patient voice strategy, BMS is vulnerable to third-party patient advocacy groups or competitors defining the patient experience narrative.

insights.label: AI/Tech Narrative
insights.text: The Microsoft partnership is an uncrowded differentiator in pharma. Developing a sustained "digital health innovation" content program positions BMS as a forward-looking leader, not a legacy company.

insights.label: LinkedIn Depth
insights.text: LinkedIn professional audience (HCPs, investors, scientists) is highly engaged. Deploying richer content — clinical data stories, exec perspectives, pipeline updates — can amplify brand authority.

insights.label: Pipeline Calendar
insights.text: Multiple Phase 2/3 readouts expected in 2026 across oncology, neuroscience, cardiovascular. Proactively mapping these to a content calendar creates 8-12 "planned" positive news moments.

insights.label: Patient Stories
insights.text: Authentic patient testimonials (with appropriate regulatory compliance) on LinkedIn and YouTube create emotional resonance that financial coverage cannot achieve — and builds payer trust.

insights.label: Trade Media Depth
insights.text: Trade media (Healthcare Research Reports, StreetInsider, STAT News equivalent) generates highest credibility per impression. Targeted pitching to these outlets amplifies scientific authority.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: January: 338 posts (baseline). February: 859 (+154%). March: 659 (-23% from peak). April: 133 partial through Apr 6.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: February spike driven by: Q4 2025 earnings release (Jan 28), SCOUT-HCM results, Scotiabank price target raise, and biopharma M&A wave coverage cascade.

insights.label: Sustainability
insights.text: March sustained at 659 (77% of Feb peak) suggests genuine organic conversation momentum, not purely event-driven flash. Positive indicator for brand relevance.

insights.label: Seasonality Watch
insights.text: Medical conference season (ASCO in late May, ASH in December) will drive future peaks. Pre-conference content building is advised to maximize coverage windows.

insights.label: Forecast
insights.text: April-May trajectory likely accelerates if Q1 2026 earnings (expected late April) and ASCO registration activity generate coverage. Model a similar Feb-level peak window.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: Jan: 7,151 · Feb: 7,431 · Mar: 6,696. Stable with a modest February peak. Far less volatile than SM volume, reflecting structural syndication coverage.

insights.label: Driver
insights.text: February uptick corresponds to earnings season (Jan 28 earnings) generating analyst commentary, financial results coverage, and follow-on discussion across trade/financial media.

insights.label: Structural vs. Earned
insights.text: The stability across months suggests most TM coverage is structural (wire syndication, financial data services) rather than earned editorial. This is high-volume but low-impact coverage.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: High structural coverage volume creates a false sense of media security. The real metric is percentage of earned editorial coverage vs. syndicated repetition.

insights.label: Implication
insights.text: Target increasing earned editorial by 20-30% by Q4 2026 through proactive media outreach to top-tier publications. Track earned vs. syndicated ratio monthly.

volTrendChart.title: Monthly Post Volume Trend
volTrendChart.subtitle: Jan–Apr 2026 · Peak: February
volTrendChart.takeaway: ↑ February peak (859 posts) driven by earnings speculation & pipeline announcements. March sustained at 659.

insights.text: LinkedIn + Radio = 76% of all social posts
Radio (736) shows DTC reach but 0 digital interactions

sentimentChart.title: Sentiment Distribution & Trend
sentimentChart.subtitle: Overall sentiment split across all SM channels
sentimentChart.takeaway: 57% neutral indicates passive brand awareness vs. active brand affinity. Pipeline & partnership events create positive spikes.

platformBreakdown.title: Platform Breakdown
platformBreakdown.subtitle: Posts & interactions per platform
platformBreakdown.takeaway: Radio has volume but zero digital engagement. LinkedIn delivers highest absolute interactions. Facebook punches above its post volume in interactions.

insights.text: 546 posts from Rank 2 sources — broadcast/wire news distribution
Only 355 posts from Rank 1 (top-tier) sources

insights.label: Volume Context
insights.text: For a large-cap pharma company, 1,989 SM posts over 90 days is moderate. Financial services and consumer brands at similar market cap typically generate 3-5× more volume.

insights.label: Channel Mix
insights.text: LinkedIn 40% + Radio 37% = 77% of volume. The remaining 23% (Twitter, Video, Facebook, Instagram) represents true social engagement channels.

insights.label: Posting Cadence
insights.text: Average 22 posts/day. LinkedIn posts average ~5-8/day. Radio represents background noise at a media monitoring level rather than active brand posting.

insights.label: Benchmark
insights.text: Peer pharma companies (Pfizer, Merck, AbbVie) typically generate 2,500-4,000 LinkedIn posts/quarter at similar scale. BMS has room to increase owned content frequency.

insights.label: Quality vs Quantity
insights.text: Focusing on quality (engagement-optimized) content over volume is the right strategy for pharma where regulatory compliance limits content types.

insights.label: Composition
insights.text: Radio posts dominate raw reach (avg 300-400K per post x 736 = ~220-280M). LinkedIn posts add ~5-15K per post. Combined radio + LinkedIn = 90%+ of total reach.

insights.label: Engagement Quality
insights.text: Higher reach ≠ higher impact. Radio reach is passive consumption; LinkedIn reach generates active professional engagement.

insights.label: Unique vs. Total
insights.text: Cumulative impressions include re-exposure. Unique audience likely 60-70% of total (180-200M unique individuals).

insights.label: Benchmark
insights.text: For pharma, 291M impressions per quarter from ~$X in media spend requires CPM analysis to optimize channel mix.

insights.label: Optimization
insights.text: Shift reach strategy: deprioritize passive Radio reach in favor of LinkedIn reach which has 1000× higher engagement conversion potential.

insights.label: Jan Baseline
insights.text: 338 posts — low activity month. Q4 2025 results not yet released; news flow relatively quiet outside Scotiabank upgrade Jan 9.

insights.label: Feb Spike
insights.text: 859 posts — driven by earnings release (Jan 28), Phase 3 SCOUT-HCM results, Microsoft AI partnership announcement, and M&A speculation cascade from industry deal wave.

insights.label: Mar Sustained
insights.text: 659 posts — decay from peak but significantly above January. Suggests BMS "found its voice" — conversation became self-sustaining as pipeline momentum continued.

insights.label: Apr Early
insights.text: 133 posts through April 6 — pace suggests full-month projection of ~500-600 posts, declining from Q1 peak but above pre-earnings baseline.

insights.label: Content Strategy
insights.text: Create a content bridge between event peaks (earnings, clinical data) and off-peak periods. Year-round editorial calendar prevents volume collapse between news events.

insights.label: LinkedIn Dominance
insights.text: 793 posts (40%) — professional networking platform drives highest-quality brand coverage and engagement. Largest share of genuine owned media.

insights.label: Radio Scale
insights.text: 736 posts (37%) — DTC broadcast creates massive passive volume. These posts are captured by media monitoring but represent broadcast ads, not social engagement.

insights.label: X/Twitter
insights.text: 253 posts (12.7%) — meaningful reach among HCPs, analysts, and journalists. Key opinion leaders active here. Quality over quantity channel.

insights.label: Video
insights.text: 100 posts (5%) — highest per-post interaction rate relative to volume among "true" social channels. Underutilized for the impact it delivers.

insights.label: Instagram
insights.text: 23 posts (1.2%) — nearly absent. Significant patient and caregiver community exists here. Major untapped opportunity for consumer pharma narrative.

insights.label: Positive (32.3%)
insights.text: 642 posts. Primary drivers: clinical trial successes, partnership announcements, analyst upgrades, oncology conference content. Science and innovation content creates positive halo.

insights.label: Neutral (57%)
insights.text: 1,133 posts. Majority of coverage is informational — DTC radio ads (neutral tonality by design), market data posts, factual press releases. Neutral ≠ bad, but high neutral rates mean low emotional resonance.

insights.label: No Sentiment (8.7%)
insights.text: 174 posts. Content where sentiment classifier couldn't determine tone — likely technical/scientific content with specialized language.

insights.label: Negative (2%)
insights.text: 40 posts. Primarily: valuation concerns, generic competition references, mixed ROE narratives. Concentrated, not widespread. Manageable.

insights.label: Platform Differential
insights.text: LinkedIn likely higher positive rate (professional enthusiasm, clinical announcements). Radio likely higher neutral. Platform-split sentiment would sharpen strategy significantly.

insights.label: Top Positive
insights.text: Camzyos SCOUT-HCM Phase 3 success, Microsoft AI partnership, Q4 2025 earnings beat narrative, Scotiabank price target raise, biopharma M&A positioning.

insights.label: Top Negative
insights.text: Patent cliff/LOE concerns, valuation debate (is $54-60 justified?), "mixed returns" narrative, generic competition in legacy products.

insights.label: Neutral Composition
insights.text: Largely DTC radio content ("No Time to Wait" ads) and financial data syndication — informational, not emotional.

insights.label: Conversion Opportunity
insights.text: Converting neutral LinkedIn content to positive is the highest-leverage intervention. Adding patient stories, clinical impact data, and exec voices to LinkedIn posts can shift 10-15% of neutrals to positive.

insights.label: Negative Mitigation
insights.text: Proactive narrative about pipeline replacements for LOE drugs (Breyanzi, Abecma, Sotyktu, Reblozyl growth stories) can directly counter the "patent cliff" negative narrative.

insights.label: The Radio Paradox
insights.text: Radio = 37% of posts, 0% of interactions. This is the most significant mismatch in the dataset — massive volume investment returning no social currency.

insights.label: LinkedIn Efficiency
insights.text: LinkedIn = 40% of posts, 64% of interactions. Highest ROI platform by a wide margin. Every LinkedIn post averages 11.8 interactions vs Radio's 0.

insights.label: Facebook Hidden Strength
insights.text: Facebook = 3.8% of posts, 13.2% of interactions. At 25 interactions/post average, Facebook may be reaching an engaged patient/consumer community that warrants more attention.

insights.label: Video Potential
insights.text: Video = 5% of posts, 8.2% of interactions (11.9 int/post average). Video content performs comparably to LinkedIn per post but is severely underutilized — only 100 posts in 90 days.

insights.label: Instagram Gap
insights.text: Instagram = 1.2% of posts, 0.2% of interactions. Extremely underperforming for the channel — either the content isn't resonating or BMS doesn't have an established Instagram audience.

insights.label: LinkedIn (793 posts)
insights.text: 9,354 interactions = 11.8 int/post. Professional B2B platform ideal for HCP, investor, and scientist audiences. Highest engagement and credibility per impression.

insights.label: Radio (736 posts)
insights.text: 0 interactions. DTC broadcast monitoring. High passive reach (avg 330K per post) but no digital engagement. Question: what is the conversion path from radio hearing to digital action?

insights.label: X/Twitter (253 posts)
insights.text: 1,982 interactions = 7.8 int/post. Real-time news distribution and KOL engagement. Key for breaking news amplification and clinical conference live coverage.

insights.label: Facebook (76 posts)
insights.text: 1,909 interactions = 25 int/post. Unexpectedly high per-post performance — suggests an engaged community that BMS is underserving with low posting frequency.

insights.label: Video + Others
insights.text: Video (100 posts, 1,192 int = 11.9/post), Instagram (23 posts, 25 int = 1.1/post). Video matches LinkedIn efficiency; Instagram severely underperforms.

insights.label: Total Interactions
insights.text: 14,466 total interactions across all SM channels. This is cumulative across 90 days — approximately 161 interactions per day across all platforms.

insights.label: LinkedIn Core
insights.text: LinkedIn's 9,354 interactions are anchored in long-form posts about pipeline, partnerships, and exec content. The top performers likely achieve 500-2,000+ interactions.

insights.label: Viral vs Passive
insights.text: No evidence of viral content (1M+ interactions) in this dataset. BMS SM is steady professional engagement, not viral consumer moments. This is appropriate for pharma but limits brand salience.

insights.label: Amplification Gap
insights.text: No data on shares/reposts specifically. But high interaction counts on LinkedIn suggest content is being amplified through professional networks — a positive chain effect.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Implement a content testing framework: A/B test post formats (image vs. carousel, data visualization vs. executive quote) on LinkedIn to identify highest-engagement content types.

insights.label: Rank 1 (355 posts)
insights.text: Top-tier sources — likely major media outlets, high-follower professionals, verified accounts. These posts deliver highest quality signal and brand impact per post.

insights.label: Rank 2 (546 posts)
insights.text: Largest single category — suggests substantial wire service / broadcast pickup. High frequency but moderate individual authority.

insights.label: Ranks 3-5
insights.text: Represents local/regional outlets, niche publications, mid-tier influencers. Important for geographic reach and niche audience penetration.

insights.label: Concentration Risk
insights.text: Top 3 ranks (1,311 posts combined) deliver majority of brand impact. If Rank 1 sources reduce BMS coverage, impact would be disproportionate.

insights.label: Strategy
insights.text: Deliberately cultivate Rank 1 source relationships: top journalists, analysts, HCP influencers on LinkedIn/Twitter. 5 Rank 1 advocates > 50 Rank 5 mentions.

volTrendChart.title: Monthly Article Volume
volTrendChart.subtitle: Jan: 7,151 · Feb: 7,431 · Mar: 6,696
volTrendChart.takeaway: February peak aligns with earnings release (Jan 28) afterglow. Volumes remarkably stable across the quarter — structural coverage, not event-driven spikes.

insights.text: Online dominates (71%) — digital-first coverage landscape
Trade media (132 articles) high-value for pharma stakeholders

sentimentChart.title: Traditional Media Sentiment Split
sentimentChart.subtitle: 30,000 articles analyzed
sentimentChart.takeaway: Overwhelmingly neutral TM coverage (78%) indicates BMS is reported on factually but not celebrated. Positive-to-negative ratio 3:1 — healthy baseline but lacks advocacy.

narratives.title: Dominant TM Narratives
narratives.subtitle: Theme analysis from top publications
narratives.takeaway: Financial/investment narrative dominates over brand/patient narrative — opportunity to shift coverage toward therapeutic impact.

items.label: Investment Story (primary):
items.text: Scotiabank, Jefferies, BMO analyst upgrades dominate Q1 framing

items.label: Pipeline Progress:
items.text: Camzyos (SCOUT-HCM), oncology compounds generate scientific credibility

items.label: M&A Landscape:
items.text: BMS named alongside Amgen/AbbVie as potential acquirer in biopharma consolidation wave

items.label: Valuation Debate:
items.text: Mixed views at $54-60 price range — "buying opportunity" vs "wait-and-see"

items.label: ROE/Profitability:
items.text: Some coverage questions sustainability of 33% ROE amid LOE pressures

insights.text: Web/Online (17,167) = syndicated financial content dominating volume
Trade (1,428) = high-value pharma/biotech industry coverage
News (2,575) = earned editorial coverage — most credibility-building

items.text: Analyst upgrades & price targets drive volume
BMY stock performance covered extensively
Net positive sentiment in this bucket
Phase 3 SCOUT-HCM (Camzyos) results
Oncology portfolio breadth recognized
AI/tech partnership elevates innovation perception
Speculation as potential acquirer
Industry consolidation context
$47B+ Q1 biopharma M&A backdrop

insights.label: Scale Context
insights.text: 30,000 articles in 90 days = 333/day. This reflects BMS's status as a major public company — constant financial, clinical, and regulatory news flow generates perpetual coverage.

insights.label: Geographic Spread
insights.text: Data shows US-centric coverage with international syndication. Local news outlets (Holliston Town News, West Jordan Journal) appear due to wire syndication, not local relevance.

insights.label: Structural vs Earned
insights.text: Estimated 60-70% structural/syndicated, 30-40% earned editorial. Improving earned editorial percentage is the key leverage point for narrative control.

insights.label: Language Mix
insights.text: Majority English, some international English outlets. Non-English coverage likely undercaptured in this dataset — potential monitoring gap for global brand health.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: The high volume creates noise. The strategic goal should be signal amplification: get fewer, better stories in top-tier publications rather than maximizing raw article count.

insights.label: Volume Driver
insights.text: High-reach outlets (MSN: 71M UMV, Yahoo Finance: 32M UMV) drive the reach concentration. A single MSN placement delivers as much reach as ~200 local news placements.

insights.label: Average Reach
insights.text: Average 18,758 per article reflects the dominance of local/aggregator outlets. Median reach likely much lower — 5,000-8,000 per article.

insights.label: Total Comparison
insights.text: 399M TM vs 291M SM. TM wins on raw reach but SM wins on engagement intensity. Combined footprint of 690M impressions is strong for Q1.

insights.label: Quality Weighting
insights.text: A better metric than total reach is quality-weighted reach: multiplying reach × editorial credibility score. Trade media at 1M reach × high credibility > aggregator at 50M reach × minimal credibility.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Build a "reach quality" tracking metric: segment reach by Tier 1 (top 20 publications), Tier 2 (trade media), Tier 3 (syndication/local). Target increasing Tier 1 reach as % of total.

insights.label: Context
insights.text: 4.5% positive is industry-typical for pharma. Financial media editorial standards require balance, limiting organic positive coverage without a story to tell.

insights.label: Positive Themes
insights.text: Investment upgrades, clinical successes, partnership announcements. These three categories drive essentially all positive TM coverage.

insights.label: Lift Opportunity
insights.text: Moving from 4.5% to 7-8% positive would add ~800-1,000 positive articles per quarter — meaningful brand impact across financial and health media audiences.

insights.label: Method
insights.text: Securing proactive feature stories (executive profiles, disease area thought leadership) in top-tier publications converts neutral coverage to positive without requiring product news.

insights.label: Target Publications
insights.text: STAT News, FiercePharma, BioPharma Dive, Modern Healthcare — these outlets generate positive sentiment when given compelling stories. They are currently underrepresented in this dataset.

insights.label: Volume Context
insights.text: 384 negative articles out of 30,000 = 1.3%. No crisis. Negative coverage is scattered and manageable — no sustained attack narrative detected.

insights.label: Primary Themes
insights.text: Valuation skepticism at $54-60 (is it overpriced?), patent expiration concerns, "mixed returns" narrative for long-term investors, generic competition mentions.

insights.label: Escalation Risk
insights.text: If a single major negative event occurs (clinical failure, safety signal, earnings miss), negative articles could triple or quadruple quickly through the same syndication mechanics that amplifies positives.

insights.label: Monitoring Priority
insights.text: Set daily negative coverage alerts. Any article reaching >100K readers with negative sentiment warrants same-day rapid response assessment.

insights.label: Structural Negative
insights.text: Some negative is structural — skeptical financial analysis is a feature of healthy markets. The goal is managing narrative, not eliminating all negative coverage.

insights.label: What Neutral Means
insights.text: 78% neutral reflects a coverage landscape dominated by factual reporting without editorial perspective — wire service summaries, financial data republication, SEC filing coverage.

insights.label: Hidden Cost
insights.text: Overwhelming neutrality means BMS is not generating emotional or reputational capital through media. Awareness without affinity is a strategic ceiling.

insights.label: Conversion Target
insights.text: If BMS could shift 10% of neutral coverage to positive (adding perspective, advocacy, emotional resonance), that's ~1,900 new positive articles — a 165% increase in positive TM volume.

insights.label: Method
insights.text: Proactive media pitching with strong story angles, providing expert spokespeople for background briefings, issuing thought leadership op-eds — these create opinion where there was only fact.

insights.label: Benchmark
insights.text: Pharma peers with strong positive TM profiles (Eli Lilly at its peak, Moderna during COVID) achieved 10-15% positive rates through aggressive narrative management. Achievable for BMS.

insights.label: Pattern
insights.text: Remarkably stable quarter-to-quarter — standard deviation of ~373 articles/month. Structural coverage > event-driven spikes explains this stability.

insights.label: February Peak
insights.text: Feb 7,431 peak = earnings-related afterglow (Jan 28 Q4 2025 results), Microsoft AI deal coverage, Scotiabank upgrade, SCOUT-HCM coverage cascade.

insights.label: March Normalization
insights.text: March 6,696 = natural post-earnings normalization. No major pipeline news in March likely explains the moderate decline from Feb levels.

insights.label: Upcoming Triggers
insights.text: Q1 2026 earnings (late April), ASCO 2026 (late May), potential Phase 3 readouts in oncology pipeline — these are predictable volume triggers to prepare content around.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Map TM volume to event calendar: identify low-news months (likely June-July) and develop proactive media pitching to maintain coverage floor and prevent brand silence periods.

insights.label: Online (71%)
insights.text: 21,278 articles from digital online sources. Primarily financial news aggregators, health media platforms, and content syndication networks. High volume, moderate quality.

insights.label: Web (26%)
insights.text: 7,761 web articles — blogs, corporate news sites, information aggregators. Strong in financial research and investor relations contexts.

insights.label: News (3%)
insights.text: 802 news articles — traditional editorial journalism. Highest quality per article but smallest volume. These are the "earned" placements with most brand impact.

insights.label: Trade (0.4%)
insights.text: 132 trade media articles — pharma/biotech specialized publications. Despite tiny volume, these reach HCPs, analysts, and industry decision makers with highest relevance.

insights.label: Strategy
insights.text: Invest in trade and traditional news coverage quality. Shift KPI from total article count to tier-weighted reach score: (News × 10 + Trade × 8 + Online × 2 + Web × 1).

insights.label: Neutral Dominance
insights.text: 78% neutral reflects the objective, factual tone of financial and health journalism. Expected for a large-cap pharma company without a major crisis or breakthrough.

insights.label: Positive Composition
insights.text: 4.5% positive = ~1,153 articles. These are anchored in: analyst upgrades, Phase 3 successes, partnership announcements, and earnings beat narratives.

insights.label: Negative Details
insights.text: 1.5% negative = ~384 articles. Themes: patent cliff, valuation debate, pipeline risk, generic competition concerns. None appear to be sustained attack campaigns.

insights.label: Positive-to-Negative
insights.text: 3:1 ratio (positive:negative) is healthy baseline. Industry benchmark for large pharma is typically 2:1 to 4:1. BMS is within healthy range.

insights.label: Other (16%)
insights.text: The "other" category (Online, Web classifications that don't map cleanly to POS/NEU/NEG) suggests some data classification nuance. Worth reviewing sentiment methodology for these articles.

insights.label: Investment Story (Primary)
insights.text: BMS is primarily framed as an investment opportunity — price targets, analyst ratings, valuation debates. This makes BMS a financial asset rather than a healthcare company in media perception.

insights.label: Pipeline Progress
insights.text: Clinical trial successes (Camzyos, oncology compounds) generate authentic scientific credibility. These stories are the most valuable for long-term brand equity with HCPs and patients.

insights.label: M&A Landscape
insights.text: BMS's potential role in the biopharma consolidation wave is a significant coverage driver. This is largely neutral/positive — investors view M&A capacity favorably.

insights.label: Valuation Debate
insights.text: A vocal minority of coverage questions whether BMS's ROE and growth profile justifies current valuation. Countering with consistent pipeline progress messaging is the recommended approach.

insights.label: Patient/Disease Impact (Missing)
insights.text: Disease burden, patient outcomes, and therapeutic impact narratives are nearly absent from TM coverage — they exist primarily in trade/specialty outlets rather than mainstream financial media.

insights.label: PRSync (522)
insights.text: Press release syndication service — represents controlled BMS messaging being distributed. High volume but low editorial authority. Signal: BMS is actively issuing communications.

insights.label: Yahoo Finance (208)
insights.text: 32M monthly unique visitors. High-reach financial platform. Content here reaches retail investors, institutional monitors, and journalists doing background research.

insights.label: MarketBeat (344)
insights.text: Financial research aggregator. High volume reflects algorithmic financial content generation around BMS stock. Significant institutional investor readership.

insights.label: Healthcare Research Reports (175)
insights.text: Specialty trade outlet. Lower volume but high-value — reaching HCP, biotech, and pharma industry decision-makers with specialized scientific and commercial framing.

insights.label: Target Tier-1 Gaps
insights.text: Notable absences from Tier 1: STAT News, FiercePharma, Wall Street Journal Health, New England Journal of Medicine. Securing coverage in these outlets would disproportionately elevate brand credibility.

insights.label: Web/Online (17,167)
insights.text: Syndicated financial content dominates — primarily aggregator platforms republishing wire content. High volume, lower editorial impact per article.

insights.label: News (2,575)
insights.text: Earned editorial coverage — written by journalists, not syndicated. These articles have the highest credibility and longest shelf life. 8.6% of total volume but much higher proportion of impact.

insights.label: Trade (1,428)
insights.text: Specialty pharma, biotech, and financial trade outlets. Smaller audience but highly relevant to R&D professionals, investors, and HCPs. Premium quality coverage.

insights.label: Magazine (107)
insights.text: 107 magazine articles — long-form, likely feature/profile coverage. Each represents significant editorial attention. Strongest for narrative development and brand positioning.

insights.label: Strategy
insights.text: Allocate comms resources to News and Trade tiers (combined 4,003 articles). These require relationship investment and proactive pitching but deliver superior brand outcomes vs. syndicated volume.

insights.label: Coverage Type
insights.text: Analyst price target changes, earnings previews/results coverage, valuation analysis, stock performance commentary. Dominant driver of TM volume.

insights.label: Sentiment
insights.text: Net positive in investment theme — more upgrades than downgrades in Q1 2026. Scotiabank, BMO, Jefferies all constructive. Retail investor confidence high.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: Investment narrative dominance means BMS brand equity is closely tied to stock performance. If BMY stock underperforms for 2 consecutive quarters, media narrative could flip quickly.

insights.label: Narrative Diversification
insights.text: Reducing dependence on investment narrative by building clinical/patient/innovation story presence would create more durable brand equity independent of short-term stock movements.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Ensure pre-earnings communications prime key investment narratives: pipeline optionality, cost discipline, strategic M&A capacity. These shape analyst note framing before publication.

insights.label: Coverage Type
insights.text: Phase 2/3 clinical trial results, regulatory submissions, FDA actions, therapy approvals, conference data presentations. Second-largest narrative in TM.

insights.label: Key Stories Q1
insights.text: SCOUT-HCM (Camzyos in adolescents), AI/lung cancer detection collaboration, targeted protein degradation pipeline, stem cell and gene therapy market context.

insights.label: Quality Premium
insights.text: Pipeline coverage tends to appear in higher-quality, higher-reach outlets (Yahoo Finance, MSN, trade media) and carries more sustained positive sentiment than investment coverage.

insights.label: Opportunity
insights.text: With 50+ compounds in development, BMS has more pipeline news to distribute than it currently publishes. Many Phase 2 readouts likely go unannounced in media-friendly formats.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Develop a "BMS Science" media program: regular updates on pipeline milestones, written for non-specialist journalists. Make it easy for generalist reporters to cover BMS science.

insights.label: Context
insights.text: Q1 2026 biopharma M&A reached $47B+ with Merck, Lilly, Biogen all closing major deals. BMS is consistently named as a logical next acquirer.

insights.label: Coverage Tone
insights.text: Mostly positive/neutral — investors view M&A capacity as a strength signal. "BMS has the balance sheet to do a deal" is framed as positive capability, not financial desperation.

insights.label: Risk
insights.text: If BMS is seen to delay in a hot M&A market, "strategic uncertainty" narratives could emerge. Analysts may question capital allocation strategy if no deal is announced by mid-2026.

insights.label: Response Strategy
insights.text: Maintain clear and consistent public messaging around business development strategy: disciplined, science-driven, value-creating. Avoid being perceived as reactive or aimless in M&A approach.

insights.label: Monitoring
insights.text: Track competitor deals weekly. If Amgen or AbbVie close a major deal that BMS was also rumored for, prepare reactive communications to maintain investor confidence.

sentimentCompare.title: Sentiment Comparison: SM vs. TM
sentimentCompare.subtitle: Positive · Neutral · Negative rates
sentimentCompare.takeaway: SM generates 7× more positive sentiment than TM. Social channels are the brand's emotional amplifier — traditional media remains predominantly informational.

insights.text: Both channels peak in February — aligned to earnings cycle
SM declines faster post-event than TM (TM sustains via syndication)

items.text: TM 15× more volume than SM (30K vs 1.9K)
SM punch above their weight on engagement quality
Radio SM posts = 37% of SM volume but 0 digital engagement
Patient narrative nearly absent from both channels
ESG/sustainability story underdeveloped in SM
TM focuses on finance; SM captures clinical/science enthusiasm
LinkedIn = primary owned channel for brand building — invest more
Use TM-confirmed M&A/pipeline moments to amplify on SM within 24hrs
Trade media engagement converts to professional credibility

reputation.title: Multi-Dimensional Reputation Scorecard
reputation.subtitle: Based on SM + TM signal analysis

scores.label: Financial Strength
scores.note: Analyst upgrades, $111B market cap, Q4 results

scores.label: Patient Trust
scores.note: DTC present but patient voice largely absent

scores.label: Brand Advocacy
scores.note: 32% SM positive but neutral dominance limits champions

scores.label: Media Presence
scores.note: 690M combined reach, TM structural coverage floor

insights.label: Aligned Themes
insights.text: Both SM and TM cover: M&A speculation, pipeline progress (Camzyos, AI/cancer), investment/analyst narrative. Thematic alignment is strong on major stories.

insights.label: Divergence
insights.text: SM carries more DTC/patient-facing content (Radio ads) while TM is purely financial/scientific. The two channels serve different audiences with limited overlap.

insights.label: Lag Observed
insights.text: SM reacts faster to news (within hours) while TM takes 24-72 hours to fully process and distribute stories. This lag is normal but creates amplification windows to exploit.

insights.label: Reputation Gaps
insights.text: Patient/caregiver narrative is essentially absent from both channels. ESG/corporate responsibility is also underdeveloped. Neither channel tells the "human impact" story.

insights.label: Integration Opportunity
insights.text: When TM breaks a major clinical or investment story, use SM within 6-12 hours to amplify with owned content — CEO LinkedIn post, infographic, patient story. Synchronize channels.

insights.label: The Gap
insights.text: SM generates 7× more positive sentiment than TM. This is a structural feature of media types, not a failure — but it has strategic implications.

insights.label: Why SM is More Positive
insights.text: Social audiences self-select for interest in BMS. People who follow BMS on LinkedIn are more likely to be enthusiastic. TM audiences are neutral by default — they didn't choose to read a BMS story.

insights.label: Brand Equity Location
insights.text: Brand emotional equity lives in SM; brand factual credibility lives in TM. Both are necessary. Neither alone is sufficient for comprehensive brand health.

insights.label: Influencing TM Sentiment
insights.text: To lift TM sentiment: (1) secure feature/profile coverage, (2) provide on-record positive spokesperson quotes, (3) generate newsworthy clinical/patient outcomes that journalists can write positively about.

insights.label: Balanced Strategy
insights.text: Don't just optimize for SM positive rate. A 5-6% TM positive rate with 3:1 ratio maintained is a realistic 12-month target that provides strong investment-grade brand health.

insights.label: SM First Mover
insights.text: Social media (especially LinkedIn and X) break and amplify BMS news within hours of announcement. Analysts tweet, executives post, investors react — all before TM has written articles.

insights.label: TM Sustainer
insights.text: Traditional media coverage extends 2-7 days post-event through editorial cycles, wire syndication, and follow-on analysis pieces. TM prolongs the life of each story.

insights.label: Window of Opportunity
insights.text: The 3-5 day window between SM peak and TM peak is critical for narrative seeding. What SM says first often shapes what TM writes next.

insights.label: Exploitation
insights.text: Pre-brief key journalists 24 hours before announcements to enable same-day TM coverage that amplifies (not follows) SM reactions. Synchronize owned SM posts with press releases.

insights.label: Monitoring
insights.text: Track daily SM mention volume as an early warning system. A spike on Day 1 (SM) that isn't matched by TM by Day 3 suggests a story that lost legs — diagnose why.

insights.label: Total Scale
insights.text: 690M combined impressions in Q1 2026. For a single pharma brand, this is enterprise-scale media presence. Represents multiple full exposures to key institutional audiences.

insights.label: Audience Quality
insights.text: TM reach quality is moderate (dominated by aggregators). SM reach quality is higher per impression (active professional audience on LinkedIn). Combined quality score is medium-high.

insights.label: Unique Audience
insights.text: Significant overlap between SM and TM audiences (same analysts, investors, journalists read both). Actual unique reach probably 400-500M after deduplication.

insights.label: Share of Voice
insights.text: With $111B market cap, BMS competes for media attention against Pfizer ($150B), Merck ($200B+), and AbbVie ($250B+). Higher market cap peers likely command proportionally larger media footprints.

insights.label: Efficiency Target
insights.text: Focus on reach efficiency: CPM improvement through better content quality, not just volume expansion. Goal: same reach, 2× engagement rate by Q3 2026.

insights.label: Positive Gap
insights.text: SM: 32.3% positive vs TM: 4.5% positive. Gap is structural (audience self-selection, content type differences) but can be partially closed through proactive TM narrative management.

insights.label: Neutral Contrast
insights.text: SM: 57% neutral vs TM: 78% neutral. TM has far more neutral coverage — wire/financial journalism defaults to factual without advocacy. Reducing TM neutral requires earned editorial investment.

insights.label: Negative Comparison
insights.text: SM: 2% negative vs TM: 1.5% negative. Both low and comparable. SM's slightly higher negative rate reflects the broader range of voices (including skeptics) on social platforms.

insights.label: Combined View
insights.text: BMS has a balanced, non-crisis media landscape. Neither channel shows alarming negative trends. The strategic opportunity is positive rate improvement, not crisis management.

insights.label: Channel Mix
insights.text: Ideal channel mix uses SM to build emotional brand equity (capitalize on higher positive rate) and TM to build factual credibility (scientific, clinical, financial validation).

insights.label: Synchronization
insights.text: Both channels peak in February — confirming that earnings and clinical news drive simultaneous SM and TM volume spikes. Channels amplify each other around major events.

insights.label: Scale Difference
insights.text: TM (7-8K articles/month) is 8-10× SM volume (660-860 posts/month). TM provides the structural coverage floor; SM provides the engagement amplification layer.

insights.label: SM Volatility
insights.text: SM volume fluctuates more dramatically (Jan 338 → Feb 859 = +154%) than TM (Jan 7.1K → Feb 7.4K = +4%). SM is more responsive to events; TM is more stable.

insights.label: Post-Event Decay
insights.text: SM decays faster post-event than TM (March SM dropped 23% from Feb peak; TM only 10%). TM syndication machine sustains coverage longer after initial events.

insights.label: Opportunity Windows
insights.text: April-June 2026 likely shows SM decline (unless pipeline events), while TM maintains structural floor. Strategic comms investment in April-May proactive pitching will prevent brand silence.

insights.label: TM Scale
insights.text: 30,000 TM articles vs 1,989 SM posts — TM is 15× SM by article count. For pharma, this ratio is normal: financial and medical media generate high organic coverage volume.

insights.label: SM Quality Premium
insights.text: Despite 1/15th the volume, SM generates more brand engagement (14,466 interactions vs essentially 0 TM interactions). SM content converts to brand action.

insights.label: Radio Distortion
insights.text: If Radio posts (736) are excluded from SM, the active social media volume drops to 1,253 posts — 24× smaller than TM. True owned SM content is a fraction of monitored SM coverage.

insights.label: Implication
insights.text: BMS has significant opportunity to grow owned SM content (corporate LinkedIn posts, @BMS tweets, owned YouTube) which would improve both volume and quality metrics.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Target 2,500 genuine owned SM posts per quarter (excluding radio) by Q4 2026 through expanded content program. Hire/activate an enterprise social media content team.

uni-ch2.title: Narrative Gaps: What's Missing
uni-ch2.sub: Absent story angles across both channels

insights.label: Patient Narrative
insights.text: Patient experience, caregiver stories, quality-of-life outcomes — nearly absent from both SM and TM. This is the gap most likely to build genuine consumer brand equity and payer trust.

insights.label: ESG/Sustainability
insights.text: Environmental, Social, and Governance narratives are underdeveloped. Given growing ESG investment mandates, this represents a gap in institutional investor targeting.

insights.label: Employee Voice
insights.text: Employee advocacy content (Glassdoor-style, LinkedIn employee stories) is minimal. Employer brand health affects talent acquisition and corporate reputation.

insights.label: Disease Education
insights.text: BMS addresses oncology, hematology, neuroscience — high-burden disease areas. Disease awareness content can build brand affinity before patients/HCPs need treatment.

insights.label: Global Narrative
insights.text: US-centric coverage with minimal international narrative. If BMS has significant international operations, building coverage in EU5, Japan, and emerging markets would expand brand footprint.

insights.label: LinkedIn Priority
insights.text: LinkedIn is BMS's highest-ROI single channel. Every additional owned post averages 11.8 interactions. Scale content investment here first — the audience is already present and engaged.

insights.label: Moment Amplification
insights.text: When TM breaks a story (clinical win, analyst upgrade, partnership), BMS has a 6-12 hour window to amplify on LinkedIn/Twitter before the moment fades. Speed is the execution gap.

insights.label: Trade Media Cultivation
insights.text: The 132 trade media articles in Q1 reach the most valuable audiences (HCPs, biotech leaders, institutional investors). A dedicated healthcare trade media relations program would compound returns.

insights.label: Integrated Calendar
insights.text: Build an integrated 12-month content calendar that maps clinical milestones, earnings events, conference seasons, and disease awareness months — coordinate SM and TM strategies in advance.

insights.label: Measurement
insights.text: Establish a unified brand health scorecard: SM engagement rate + TM positive % + combined reach quality score. Review monthly, present to leadership quarterly.

insights.label: Innovation
insights.text: Strong — Microsoft AI partnership, 50+ compound pipeline, protein degradation technology. Well-covered in both SM and TM. Score: 7/10.

insights.label: Financial Strength
insights.text: Strong — analyst upgrades, $111B market cap, strong Q4 results. Dominant TM narrative. Score: 8/10.

insights.label: Pipeline Credibility
insights.text: Good — SCOUT-HCM success, multiple Phase 3 assets. Could be stronger with more proactive pipeline communications. Score: 7/10.

insights.label: Patient Trust
insights.text: Weak — "No Time to Wait" DTC present but patient voice largely absent. No patient storytelling detected in SM data. Score: 4/10.

insights.label: Brand Advocacy
insights.text: Moderate — 32% positive SM rate suggests engaged professional advocates, but neutral dominance means limited brand champions. Score: 5/10.

items.title: Double LinkedIn Content Investment
items.description: LinkedIn is 40% of SM volume but drives 64% of all interactions. Deploy deeper editorial — pipeline explainers, clinical data storytelling, executive thought leadership — to convert passive followers into brand advocates.

items.title: Capitalize on Pipeline Milestone Moments
items.description: SCOUT-HCM, AI/lung cancer detection, and protein degradation pipeline are generating strong organic coverage. Build proactive content calendars around ASCO, ASH, and regulatory milestones to pre-seed positive narrative.
items.kpi: Target: each milestone drives 200+ TM articles, 500+ SM posts within 7 days

items.title: Leverage Microsoft AI Partnership for Innovation Positioning
items.description: The Microsoft/AI lung cancer deal is a signal investors and innovators respond to. Create a sustained “BMS + AI” content series targeting LinkedIn, X, and trade media to anchor BMS as a tech-forward pharma company.
items.kpi: Target: “BMS AI” mentions grow 3× by Q4 2026. 5 Tier 1 placements/quarter

items.title: Optimize or Redirect Radio/DTC Spend
items.description: Radio accounts for 37% of SM posts (736 posts) but generates 0 digital interactions. Add a digital/social retargeting layer to capture radio-exposed audiences and create a measurable funnel.
items.kpi: Target: 500 social actions per 100 radio impressions within 30 days

items.title: Reduce Neutral Content Volume in TM
items.description: 78% of TM coverage is neutral — purely informational. Develop a proactive media engagement strategy targeting top financial publications with story angles that drive opinion and advocacy, not just reportage.
items.kpi: Target: TM positive rate Q2: 5% → Q3: 6% → Q4: 7%

items.title: Build Patient/Caregiver Voice in Social
items.description: The social conversation is dominated by financial/professional audiences. The patient narrative — which drives credibility with payers, advocacy groups, and regulators — is nearly absent from monitored data.
items.kpi: Target: 100 patient-focused posts/quarter on Instagram/YouTube by Q4 2026

items.title: M&A Speculation Risk
items.description: BMS is named as a potential acquirer in the biopharma M&A wave. If a deal rumor breaks inaccurately or a failed bid surfaces, negative sentiment could spike. Prepare rapid-response comms playbook for M&A scenarios.
items.kpi: Conduct M&A scenario planning exercise Q2 2026. Real-time alert activation

items.title: Valuation Credibility Under Pressure
items.description: A vocal minority of financial media questions BMS valuation sustainability at $54–60 given LOE headwinds. Maintain a steady cadence of investor-facing pipeline updates to counter this narrative.
items.kpi: Alert threshold: negative TM exceeds 3% any single week → immediate response

items.title: Competitor SOV Erosion
items.description: With Merck, AbbVie, Amgen, AstraZeneca all generating comparable M&A/pipeline buzz simultaneously, BMS risks losing relative share of voice. Track weekly SOV against Merck, AbbVie, and Pfizer as primary benchmarks.
items.kpi: Alert threshold: BMS SOV drops below 12% → trigger reactive campaign

insights.label: Scale (3)
insights.text: Double LinkedIn investment, capitalize on pipeline milestones, leverage Microsoft AI partnership. All three are opportunity amplifiers — low risk, high ROI, executable within 90 days.

insights.label: Fix (3)
insights.text: Radio/DTC optimization, TM neutral reduction, patient voice activation. These address structural gaps in BMS’s media footprint that limit brand credibility with key audiences.

insights.label: Monitor (3)
insights.text: M&A speculation, valuation pressure, competitor SOV. These are not crises — but unchecked, any one could materially shift BMS’s media standing within a single news cycle.

insights.label: Priority Order
insights.text: Recommended execution sequence: (1) LinkedIn scale-up (immediate, Q2), (2) Radio optimization (immediate, Q2), (3) Pipeline calendar (Q2 planning, Q3 execution), (4) Patient voice program (Q3), (5) Monitor/alert setup (Q2).

insights.label: Why Scale First
insights.text: Scaling proven channels has higher certainty of ROI than fixing broken ones. LinkedIn, pipeline storytelling, and AI narrative are all validated — the investment thesis is proven, now execute at higher volume.

insights.label: Budget Implication
insights.text: Scale actions require primarily human capital investment (content creators, editors, PR managers) rather than media spend. Estimated $500K–$1M incremental annual investment for full execution of all three.

insights.label: 90-Day Wins
insights.text: LinkedIn frequency increase can show results in 30-60 days. Pipeline calendar requires Q2 planning but delivers Q3 results. Microsoft AI series can begin publishing within 45 days of approval.

insights.label: Why Fix Matters
insights.text: Fix actions don’t just improve metrics — they prevent competitor exploitation of gaps. A BMS that doesn’t tell the patient story cedes that narrative to disease advocacy groups who may not align with corporate messaging.

insights.label: Compliance Note
insights.text: Patient storytelling in pharma requires MLR (medical, legal, regulatory) review. Build the compliance pathway first (Q2) before launching content programs (Q3). This is a regulatory discipline, not a creative one.

insights.label: Quick Win
insights.text: The radio/DTC call-to-action addition is a 2-week creative execution that costs almost nothing and creates measurable digital attribution for a $5M+ broadcast investment.

recs-monitor.title: Monitor Track: Watch for Emerging Risks

insights.label: Not a Crisis — Yet
insights.text: None of the three monitor items are active crises. M&A speculation is normal at BMS’s market cap, valuation concerns are minority view, and SOV is stable. The risk is inaction if conditions change.

insights.label: Alert Infrastructure
insights.text: Implement real-time media monitoring alerts for: “BMS acquires” (M&A), negative TM >3% weekly (valuation), BMS SOV <12% (competitor). Set up a weekly media risk dashboard reviewed by Corporate Affairs.

insights.label: Scenario Planning
insights.text: Conduct a Q2 2026 communications scenario planning session. Pre-draft 3 holding statements per risk scenario. Designate a rapid-response team for sub-2-hour media inquiry turnaround.

insights.label: Evidence
insights.text: LinkedIn generates 64% of all SM interactions from 40% of posts. ROI per post (11.8 avg interactions) is significantly higher than any other channel. The audience is already present and engaged.

insights.label: Action
insights.text: Increase LinkedIn post frequency from ~9/day to 15–20/day through expanded content types: clinical data explainers, executive thought leadership, pipeline updates, employee stories.

insights.label: Content Mix
insights.text: Recommended split: 30% clinical/scientific, 25% executive/company voice, 20% pipeline/R&D, 15% partnership/innovation, 10% patient/disease awareness.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: 18,000+ interactions/quarter (from current 9,354). 85% of followers reached. 5% interaction rate per post.

insights.label: Investment
insights.text: Estimated 2–3 dedicated LinkedIn content creators/editors with pharma-grade compliance review process. ROI measured in brand affinity, HCP reach, and investor sentiment uplift.

insights.label: Insight
insights.text: SCOUT-HCM, Microsoft AI deal, and analyst upgrades drove the February SM/TM peaks. These were reactive amplifications. A proactive content calendar converts reactive to strategic.

insights.label: Identify Triggers
insights.text: Map all known 2026 milestones: Phase 3 readouts, FDA PDUFA dates, conference presentations (ASCO, ASH, AHA, ESMO), investor days, earnings dates. ~12–16 identified milestone windows per year.

insights.label: Pre-seed Content
insights.text: 2 weeks before each milestone, seed educational content (disease burden, treatment landscape) on LinkedIn to prime audience. Day-of: press release + LinkedIn post + Twitter thread. Days 2–7: follow-on content with analyst reactions.

insights.label: Trade Media
insights.text: Pre-brief top 5 trade outlets (FiercePharma, STAT News, BioPharma Dive) 24 hours before announcements. Increases chance of day-one editorial vs. wire syndication only.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: each major milestone drives 200+ TM articles, 500+ SM posts, 5,000+ interactions within 7 days of announcement.

rec-3.title: Leverage Microsoft AI Partnership for Innovation Positioning

insights.label: Context
insights.text: The Microsoft lung cancer AI partnership generated strong earned media and social engagement. This narrative differentiates BMS in a field of legacy drug companies.

insights.label: Content Series
insights.text: Launch “BMS Science + Technology” content series: monthly LinkedIn articles on AI applications in R&D, interviews with data science team, patient benefit framing of AI tools.

insights.label: Media Targeting
insights.text: Pitch AI-in-pharma stories to: MIT Technology Review, Wired, STAT News, Forbes Health, FierceBiotech. These non-traditional pharma outlets reach new audiences (tech, investor, talent).

insights.label: Partnership Visibility
insights.text: Activate Microsoft marketing to co-amplify the partnership story. Microsoft has 19M LinkedIn followers — a co-authored post would dramatically extend BMS reach.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: “BMS AI” branded mentions grow 3× from current Q1 level by Q4 2026. 5 Tier 1 media placements on AI-in-pharma angle per quarter.

insights.label: Problem
insights.text: 736 radio posts in SM monitoring = significant DTC broadcast spend. 0 digital interactions = zero measurable social ROI. The two channels are completely siloed.

insights.label: Quick Win
insights.text: Add social call-to-action to radio ads: “Search ‘No Time to Wait’ and follow us on LinkedIn for the latest on AFib treatment.” Direct broadcast audiences to owned digital channels.

insights.label: Retargeting
insights.text: Implement programmatic retargeting of radio-exposed audiences via geo-data and audio ad measurement tools. Serve digital ads to people who heard the radio spot within 7 days.

insights.label: URL/QR
insights.text: Add vanity URL (notimetowait.com) or QR code to all radio creative. Track digital visits from radio exposure to create a measurable funnel: radio → digital → conversion.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: 500 social actions (follows, website visits, video views) per 100 radio broadcast impressions within 30 days. If unachievable, reassess radio budget allocation.

rec-5.title: Reduce Neutral Content Volume in TM

insights.label: Mechanism
insights.text: Most TM neutrality is structural — wire syndication creates informational content without opinion. To change this, BMS needs to give journalists compelling stories with emotional angles.

insights.label: Spokesperson Program
insights.text: Develop a roster of BMS scientists, executives, and patients who provide on-record quotes for journalists. A good quote transforms a neutral wire pickup into a positive featured article.

insights.label: Feature Pitching
insights.text: Identify 5 Tier 1 journalists covering pharma/biotech at major outlets. Build relationships with quarterly briefings. Offer exclusive data/access for feature stories that go beyond earnings coverage.

insights.label: Op-Ed Strategy
insights.text: Commission executive op-eds in STAT News, Wall Street Journal Health, Forbes Health on: precision medicine, AI in oncology, the future of cardiovascular treatment.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target TM positive rate progression: Q2: 5%, Q3: 6%, Q4: 7%. Corresponding neutral reduction: 78% → 70% by Q4 2026.

insights.label: Gap
insights.text: Patient and caregiver voices are essentially absent from monitored SM data. For a company treating cancer, heart disease, and neurological conditions, this is a credibility gap competitors can exploit.

insights.label: Compliance Path
insights.text: Regulatory-compliant patient storytelling on social media is achievable with proper legal/medical/regulatory review. Use pre-reviewed testimonial frameworks and video formats.

insights.label: Partnerships
insights.text: Partner with patient advocacy organizations (American Cancer Society, American Heart Association, National MS Society) to co-create and amplify patient-centered content.

insights.label: Platform
insights.text: Instagram and YouTube are underutilized BMS channels with strong patient/caregiver communities. Invest here specifically for patient narrative — different audience from LinkedIn.

insights.label: KPI
insights.text: Target: 100 patient-focused social posts per quarter on Instagram/YouTube by Q4 2026. Monitor Net Promoter Score with patient community surveys.

insights.label: Trigger Conditions
insights.text: M&A risk escalates if: (1) a specific target company is leaked to media, (2) BMS files an SEC notice, (3) a competitor deal falls apart and BMS is named as alternate buyer.

insights.label: Rapid Response
insights.text: Prepare 3 holding statements: (1) “We don’t comment on rumors,” (2) “We have a disciplined M&A strategy focused on scientific value,” (3) Full announcement for actual deal. Activate within 2 hours of media inquiry.

insights.label: Social Monitoring
insights.text: Set real-time alerts for: “BMS acquires,” “Bristol-Myers deal,” “BMY merger.” Include financial media and Twitter/X monitoring for analyst speculation before it goes to print.

insights.label: Analyst Relations
insights.text: Maintain active dialogue with key analysts (Scotiabank, BMO, Jefferies) to ensure coverage of BMS M&A activity reflects accurate strategic context, not speculation.

insights.label: Timeline
insights.text: Conduct M&A communications scenario planning exercise Q2 2026. Identify top 3 likely deal scenarios and pre-draft stakeholder communications for each.

insights.label: Current Status
insights.text: 384 negative TM articles focus on valuation concerns and LOE risks. Not a crisis — but a structural negative narrative that could amplify if Q2/Q3 revenue disappoints.

insights.label: Proactive Counter
insights.text: The best defense against LOE coverage is a compelling growth pipeline story. Every Phase 3 success, BD deal, or growth brand milestone should be positioned as “the next chapter.”

insights.label: Growth Brand Amplification
insights.text: Breyanzi, Abecma, Sotyktu, Reblozyl, Augtyro are BMS growth drivers post-Revlimid LOE. Develop a “Next Generation BMS” narrative featuring these brands prominently in communications.

insights.label: Investor Communications
insights.text: At investor days and earnings calls, proactively quantify pipeline value. Show analysts that pipeline NPV exceeds LOE revenue impact. Third-party analyst validation is most credible.

insights.label: Alert Threshold
insights.text: If negative TM exceeds 3% for any single week (vs current 1.5%), trigger comms team response. If a single negative article reaches >5M readers, same-day response required.

insights.label: Current Landscape
insights.text: Merck, AbbVie, Amgen, Pfizer, AstraZeneca all generating comparable M&A/pipeline coverage in Q1 2026. BMS holds its own currently but active monitoring is essential.

insights.label: SOV Measurement
insights.text: Establish weekly SOV tracking: BMS mentions vs. Merck + AbbVie + Pfizer + Amgen. SOV should be maintained at 15–20% of the total “big pharma” conversation.

insights.label: Trigger Events
insights.text: A competitor FDA approval, major M&A announcement, or clinical breakthrough could rapidly shift SOV. BMS needs a 48-hour response protocol to maintain relevance in these windows.

insights.label: Differentiation Strategy
insights.text: In commodity news moments, BMS can differentiate by commenting through scientific/patient lens rather than financial. This creates distinct brand voice vs. finance-first competitors.

insights.label: Reporting Cadence
insights.text: Monthly SOV report to CMO/Corporate Affairs VP. Quarterly SOV trend to executive leadership. Alert threshold: BMS SOV drops below 12% → trigger reactive comms campaign.
